seoTitle: Myrtle Mountain vs Myrtleville — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Myrtle Mountain and Myrtleville. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Myrtle Mountain vs Myrtleville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Myrtle Mountain and Myrtleville.
heading: Myrtle Mountain vs Myrtleville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Myrtle Mountain, New South Wales and Myrtleville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Myrtleville has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Myrtle Mountain (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Myrtle Mountain or Myrtleville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Myrtle Mountain has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Myrtleville scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Myrtle Mountain scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Myrtleville scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Myrtle Mountain or Myrtleville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Myrtleville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
